title: Software Developer
achievements: Delivered 4–5 production web and mobile applications end-to-end for researcher, data-scientist and operational-user teams.
Integrated LLMs and foundational models to automate workflows previously handled manually by researchers.
responsibilities: Designed and built backend services and REST APIs in Node.js and Python, integrating PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Milvus and Neo4j.
Built RAG pipelines and LLM-enabled data workflows for model training, fine-tuning and inference.
Implemented CI/CD with GitHub Actions and container deployments via Docker and OpenStack, improving release consistency.
Conducted API design reviews and produced technical docs via Swagger and GitHub Wiki.
Mentored an incoming teammate through onboarding and provided semester-long guidance to student contributors.

title: Application Developer
achievements: Kau Mau Te Wehi Award (2019) — verified ~10% improvement in annual disaster-recovery exercise efficiency.
Replaced manual Excel workflows with a Java / Spring Boot microservices disaster-recovery platform.
responsibilities: Performed MS SQL Server schema updates and data migrations with zero data-loss incidents across DR exercises.
Supported containerised deployments with Docker and Red Hat OpenShift and helped configure the CI/CD pipeline.
Translated operational requirements from business stakeholders into application features.
Investigated and resolved critical issues during live DR exercises under operational pressure.
Produced technical and end-user documentation to support adoption by frontline teams.

title: Research Fellow
responsibilities: Contributed to an AI-powered browser extension improving web accessibility for users with visual impairments.
Participated in user research, prototyping, and early-stage product evaluation.

title: Research Software Engineer Intern
responsibilities: Researched and developed a computer-vision solution to automate 2D floor-plan analysis, cutting manual labelling effort.
Collaborated with domain experts to design, test and refine models; presented results for integration.
